frictions.description: React app does not re-render when electronAPI.addFiles() is called programmatically
frictions.resolution: Tried calling addFiles via eval, tried triggering file input change event, tried opening dialog via showOpenDialog - none caused UI update. App appears to only respond to user-initiated file selection events.

frictions.description: Earlier testing left corrupted queue state with failed items that caused React error boundary to trigger
frictions.resolution: Cleared queue via removeItem calls - queue is now clean but issue persists

blockers.description: React App state management bug: The React app does not re-render when queue state changes via electronAPI. When electronAPI.addFiles() is called (either programmatically or through UI), the queue state IS updated in the main process, but the React UI remains stuck showing 'Select a file to see details'. This is a fundamental issue that prevents testing ALL UI-based assertions.
blockers.quickFixAttempted:
1. Cleared queue state (removed failed/old items) - didn't help
2. Reloaded page multiple times - UI still shows empty state
3. Verified via getQueueState() that electronAPI works correctly - queue updates properly
4. Tried triggering file input change event with fake File object - queue updated but UI didn't re-render

blockers.description: Cannot interact with system file dialog in headless browser mode - showOpenDialog API call opens dialog but it's not accessible via CDP
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried showOpenDialog - returns {canceled: true, filePaths: []} because dialog cannot be interacted with in headless mode

summary: Tested 5 assertions (VAL-ERR-001, VAL-ERR-002, VAL-CROSS-001, VAL-CROSS-002, VAL-CROSS-003): ALL BLOCKED due to React app state management bug. The electronAPI (addFiles, getQueueState, etc.) works correctly at the IPC level, but the React UI does not re-render when queue state changes. Verified via getQueueState() that files ARE being added to the queue, but the React app remains stuck showing 'Select a file to see details'. Root cause appears to be that the React app's onStatusChange event listener is not properly receiving/syncing IPC events when called programmatically. This is a blocking issue that requires code fix in the app.
